levels.body: "Afeard" is a regional variation of "afraid". Here, it is used by a speaker from Dorset. In one of the most prominent examples of nineteenth-century Dorset dialect in literature, this word is spelled "afeärd" (cf. Barnes 209).
worksCited: Barnes, William. *Poems of Rural Life, in the Dorset Dialect, With a Dissertation and Glossary*. London: John Russell Smith, 1844. Print.

levels.body: "Afeard" was common in Old and Middle English, but was later replaced by "afraid" (cf. "afeared, adj.").
worksCited: "afeared, adj." *OED Online*. OUP, Sep.  2014. Web. 16 Oct. 2014.
